subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear identification of the subject area, company context and timeframe
Well-stated, focused research question with dual variables guiding the investigation
Adequate mixed-methods approach named and applied (quantitative financial data, qualitative consumer sentiment)
weaknesses: Introduction contains some repetitive background and occasional anecdotal claims lacking immediate citation
Limited justification for the selection and reliability of particular sources or methods
âBrand imageâ variable not precisely defined or operationalized
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation communicates the topic adequately with sufficient explanation and a clearly focused research question that shapes the structure, placing it in the 3â4 band. Methodology is generally relevant but lacks in-depth justification for source selection. Overall, a solid but not fully integrated presentation of focus and method merits 4/6.

strengths: Effective integration of the BCG matrix and profit-margin comparisons to support analysis
Consistent use of key business terminology (e.g. âcash cowâ, psychological pricing)
Broad range of relevant source material (academic journals, industry reports, legal sources) applied to support points
weaknesses: Occasional misuse of terminology (e.g. âprecipitationâ vs âperceptionâ), and minor factual inaccuracies (pre-2018 Activision naming)
Some numerical claims and figures lack clear sourcing or have conflicting data
In-text citation style is inconsistent, leading to occasional missing references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and understanding are generally clear and coherent, with mostly appropriate application of source material and effective use of disciplinary concepts. Occasional errors and gaps in citation keep it from the highest band, justifying a mid-band score of 4/6.

strengths: Research draws on a wide range of quantitative and qualitative sources all relevant to the research question
Balanced, reasoned argument linking financial performance and brand-image impacts
Contextualization of legal and ethical considerations (e.g. Belgian gambling regulations) enhances evaluation
weaknesses: Analytical causal links (pricing to brand damage) are sometimes asserted rather than fully demonstrated
Alternative perspectives and source reliability are insufficiently critiqued
Recommendations remain general with limited actionable detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates good research quality and clear relevance, with well-supported analysis and a balanced argument. However, evaluation is only partially critical and depth of analysis has some gaps. Overall performance sits in the 7â9 band; a score of 8/12 reflects solid critical thinking with room to deepen critique and recommendations.

strengths: Clearly organized structure with title page, table of contents, numbered sections and figures
Consistent use of headings and logical section flow matching the research question
Basic formatting requirements (word count, pagination, works-cited list) are met
weaknesses: Citation style and reference formatting are inconsistent
Figure captions and labeling occasionally ambiguous or non-standard
Some images appear low resolution and there are minor proofreading lapses
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs structure meets requirements and supports readability, placing it in the top band for structure. Layout and formatting show effort but contain inconsistencies, situating it in the mid-band overall. A score of 3/4 balances strengths in organization with weaknesses in consistency and detail.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question is explicitly stated, time-bounded (2010-2024) and framed around two measurable variablesâprofit and brand imageâshowing a clear attempt at focus.  It is referenced throughout the essay and shapes the structure (financial impact first, brand image second).

Nevertheless, âbrand imageâ remains vaguely defined and the methods for measuring it (Steam reviews, media articles) are not set out with precision, leading to partial rather than full alignment between question and discussion.  This places the strand in the 3-4 band.

The student presents a balanced conclusion that recognises financial gain *and* reputational cost, referencing earlier evidence.  Ethical and regulatory repercussions are considered, showing some critical evaluation.

However, the discussion rarely challenges the reliability of sources, alternative interpretations are thin, and recommended responses for Activision remain general.  Argumentation is well-developed but only partially critical, best fitting the 4-6 band at its lower end.

The student demonstrates sound understanding of business concepts such as BCG matrix, harvest strategy, pricing psychology and profit-margin analysis, and links these to Activisionâs behaviour.  Key terminology (e.g., âcash cowâ, âfree-to-playâ) is generally used correctly.

Nonetheless, certain factual errors (e.g., conflating Activision with Activision Blizzard pre-2018) and over-simplifications show that knowledge, while clear, is not always fully accurate or deeply substantiated by sources.  This aligns with the 3-4 band.

Business-management terminology is employed throughoutâBCG quadrants, revenue distribution, profit margins, psychological pricing, product life cycleâindicating reasonable command of the disciplineâs lexicon.

There are, however, occasional lapses (e.g., misuse of âharvestingâ without cost figures, ambiguous use of âoperational efficiencyâ) and minor stylistic inconsistencies.  Overall usage is âmostly accurateâ, placing it in the middle band.

Analysis moves beyond description: revenue trends are connected to micro-transaction strategy; BCG mapping is used to justify portfolio decisions; ethical concerns are weighed against profitability.  Assertions are usually backed by evidence.

Yet causal links (e.g., from pricing to brand-image damage) are sometimes asserted rather than fully demonstrated, and numerical comparisons with competitors lack depth.  The analysis is clear and generally supported but not consistently rigorous, positioning it mid-band (4).

Figures, quotations and statistics from company filings, Statista and academic journals are generally relevant and are integrated into the narrative (e.g., revenue shares, profit margins).  In most cases the data support the point being made.

However, some numerical claims (share-price timeline, production-cost estimate of cosmetic packs) are presented without clear sourcing or appear questionable, and in-text citations are occasionally missing.  The application is therefore âmostlyâ rather than âclearlyâ appropriate, justifying a mid-band mark.

The student outlines a mixed-method approach (quantitative financial data, qualitative consumer sentiment) and lists a variety of sources such as annual reports, surveys and academic papers.  Basic tools (BCG matrix, PLC, pricing strategies) are named and later applied.

Yet the essay does not justify why particular sources were chosen over alternatives, and there is limited critical comment on reliability or limitations.  The range is acceptable but evidence of *effective* and *informed* selection is only moderate, so the work fits the 3-4 band.

The essay draws on financial statements, market data, academic studies and media reports that are clearly related to the research question.  Quantitative evidence (revenue percentages, profit margins) is linked to qualitative sources (consumer surveys, media criticism), showing breadth and relevance.

While some data credibility issues exist, the overall body of research demonstrates clear alignment with, and support for, the investigation, meriting placement in the 4-6 band close to its upper end.

Basic formatting conventions are met: word count, pagination, and tables/figures are present.  In-text referencing is attempted and a works-cited list is included.

Nonetheless, citation style is inconsistent, figure labelling occasionally ambiguous, and some images appear stretched or low-resolution.  These weaknesses place layout in the 1-2 band rather than the top band.

The student clearly identifies the subject area (Business Management), names the company, franchise, phenomenon and time frame, and explains why micro-transactions are commercially and socially significant.  Background statistics and industry context in the introduction help the reader understand *why* the issue matters. 

However, parts of the introduction are repetitive, some contextual details (e.g. history of monetisation) are only loosely linked to the stated focus and several claims are anecdotal or lack immediate citation.  Because the explanation is solid but not consistently concise or fully integrated, the work fits best in the âadequately communicated with sufficient explanationâ band rather than the highest band.

The report follows the expected EE structure with title page, table of contents, numbered sections, figures and a works-cited list.  Headings logically mirror the research question (financial effects first, brand image second), facilitating reader navigation.

Minor issuesâsuch as occasional abrupt section breaks and some duplication between introduction and methodologyâdo not seriously hinder comprehension, so the structure is âclearly appropriateâ.

annotations.comment: The title of the investigation is prominently stated at the start of the report, effectively orienting the reader to the focus of the study.
The caption âFig 1. Activision Blizzardâs Share Priceâ lacks consistent labeling format; consider standardizing figure numbering and placement of captions below charts.
The BCG matrix (Figure 2) is effectively integrated, using clear categories to map Call of Duty titles against market growth and share, demonstrating apt application of theory.
The table of contents is comprehensively organized with consistent formatting, facilitating easy navigation through the essayâs sections.
The term âprecipitationâ in describing negative emotions appears to be a misuse; consider replacing with âperceptionâ or âfeelingâ to maintain disciplinary accuracy.
Comparing Activisionâs 25.03% profit margin to the industry average of 10% and to EAâs 16.87% demonstrates effective quantitative analysis of operational efficiency.
The claim about the industry-average profit margin of 10% is presented without citation; provide a reference to support this benchmark.
The student provides a clear word count at the start of the paper, fulfilling the formatting requirement and aiding transparency in scope management.
Figure 1âs data trail is unclear: the flat share-price values from 2015â2020 conflict with narrative stating merger effects in 2018. Cite data origins and clarify timeline.
The statistic â31% of Steam reviews positiveâ lacks a direct citation; include a source link or reference to validate this quantitative point.
The discussion of regulatory challenges is strong but omits consideration of other markets (e.g., China or Australia); broadening this scope would enhance the global relevance.
The research question is explicitly framed with a clear time span and dual variables (profit and brand image), providing strong focus to guide the investigation.
The comparison to EAâs pay-to-win controversies provides relevant context, enriching the evaluation of Activisionâs microtransaction strategy.
Linking Belgiumâs gambling regulations to Activisionâs microtransaction model effectively contextualizes potential legal risks in the ethical evaluation.
The conclusion succinctly reiterates the studyâs key insights on microtransaction evolution and strategic balance, reinforcing coherence.
The introduction contains extensive historical background that, while informative, lacks direct linkage to the research question and sometimes reads as narrative rather than integrated explanation.
The sentence describing Activisionâs parent company is left incomplete, indicating a lapse in proofreading that interrupts clarity in the background section.
The methodology summary reiterates earlier content without justifying selection criteria for sources; providing rationale for source choices would strengthen methodological rigour.
The claim about microtransaction price increases from $2 to $30 is presented without citation; a direct source reference is needed to validate this significant assertion.
Axis labels for the BCG matrix are described as âHigh share on the left,â which conflicts with conventional matrix orientation; clarify your axis definitions to avoid conceptual confusion.
Figure 4 vividly illustrates one of the earliest cosmetic packs, effectively grounding the discussion of consumer scepticism in concrete imagery.

The description of a âharvesting strategyâ lacks quantitative metrics or cost data to substantiate why marketing and operating expenses were reduced; provide evidence or figures supporting this claim.
Figure 3âs three pie charts clearly illustrate the shift in revenue distribution, and the accompanying table reinforces the visual analysis with precise percentage values.
The list of psychological pricing tactics (bundling, scarcity, in-game currency) demonstrates strong understanding of consumer behaviour concepts and links them to microtransaction design.
The works-cited list demonstrates breadth, drawing on academic journals, industry reports, legal sources, and media articles, showing effective research scope.
The discussion on microtransactionsâ strategic value is largely narrative; incorporating evaluative critique or alternative perspectives would deepen the critical analysis.
The assertion regarding exploitation of younger audiences is plausible but would benefit from empirical evidence or survey data to support the claim.
The conclusion summarizes findings effectively but offers few actionable recommendations for Activisionâs strategic response, limiting the evaluative depth.
Reference entries show inconsistent formatting (e.g., use of italics, placement of accessed dates); standardize citation style to improve presentation.
The balanced argument between financial success and reputational cost demonstrates sound critical thinking in evaluating brand image impacts.
